mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, triggering negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.2pp over 3 months, invalidating the contraction signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when RSAFS and HOUST both exceed their 12-month moving averages, with out-of-sample accuracy below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates to higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to precede positive 20-day SPX returns during periods where CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3.0% annually, indicating supply constraints override demand signals.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real corporate margins and prompts expectations of tighter monetary policy, causing negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns remain positive in > 60% of months where CPIAUCSL MoM exceeds 0.4%, or the signal shows zero predictive power in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, supporting higher wage growth and consumer spending that lifts S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS and UNRATE signal yields a negative or flat 20-day forward return distribution when tested across multiple 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth directly increases aggregate corporate revenues and profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 growth rates show no statistically significant positive correlation with subsequent 60-day SPX returns over a 5-year backtest period.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in industrial production signals immediate supply chain disruptions and falling business investment, triggering negative S&P 500 returns within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances where INDPRO declines by more than 0.5% month-over-month, invalidating the contraction signal.
